channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio governor signs bill to charge public for police video footage
item.description: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) signed a new measure into law earlier this week that permits police departments to charge the public to release camera footage from its officers — including dash, body or surveillance video.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza kill at least 21, hospital workers say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 21 people, including at least two children, on Saturday in Gaza, hospital staff said, while a new effort at ceasefire talks was underway in Qatar after nearly 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Drivers skid and crash as wintery mix grips central US then moves east
item.description: Road conditions were deteriorating Saturday in the central U.S. as a mix of snow, ice and plunging temperatures arrived, with forecasts calling for the dreaded combo to spread eastward in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Take these steps to protect yourself from winter weather dangers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Winter weather brings various hazards that people have to contend with to keep warm and safe. These dangers can include carbon monoxide poisoning, hypothermia and frozen pipes that can burst and make homes unlivable.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Filmmaker and husband to Aubrey Plaza, Jeff Baena, dies by suicide: report
item.description: Film writer and director Jeff Baena, who is also known for his marriage to Hollywood actress Aubrey Plaza, has died at the age of 47, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Paleontologists learn from rare 'Teen Rex' specimen
item.description: Details about the juvenile Tyrannosaurus rex fossil discovered by three boys in North Dakota are beginning to take shape, including its size, age and how its body worked.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans attacker’s apparent loyalty to Islamic State group highlights persistent threat of lone wolf terrorism
item.description: The deadly Jan. 1, 2025, attack in New Orleans serves as a reminder of the persistent threat to the U.S. from individuals inspired by extremist Islamist groups.
item.dc:creator: Sara Harmouch, American University, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans attacker had suspected bomb materials at home, reserved truck weeks ago, officials say
item.description: Law enforcement officials say the man who rammed a truck into a New Year's crowd had suspected bomb-making materials at his home and reserved the rental vehicle used in the attack weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Jim Mustian, Kevin Mcgill And Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected illegal immigrant's fake bomb threat shuts down SC highway
item.description: A suspected illegal immigrant was arrested in South Carolina after officials say he made a fake bomb threat during a traffic stop which ultimately led to a section of the I-85 being shut down for nearly five hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother, son given lengthy prison terms for roles in the killings of 8 Ohio family members
item.description: Two more family members convicted in the killings of eight members of an Ohio family received lengthy prison terms on Friday for their roles in the 2016 shootings, as prosecutions near completion in what has been described as the most heinous crime in modern Ohio history.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois man finds decades-old gift hidden in wall of childhood home
item.description: Waiting for holiday gifts can feel agonizing, but for one suburban man, the wait took about 46 years longer than usual.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Congresswoman calls for Fort Liberty whistleblowers to come forward after NOLA, Vegas attacks
item.description: Florida Rep. Anna Paulina Luna is urging anyone who encountered the New Orleans terrorist or Las Vegas bomber at Fort Liberty, formerly Fort Bragg, to come forward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington received the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington were awarded the nation’s highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know as snow, freezing rain and bitter cold heads through much of the US
item.description: The National Weather Service says a major winter storm is forecast to produce heavy snow and significant ice starting in the central U.S. and moving east.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral has started. Here's what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter are set to take place in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sunday school class with Jimmy Carter: What it was like
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — It never got old. No matter how many times one crammed into the modest sanctuary at Maranatha Baptist Church, there was always some wisdom to be gleaned from the measured, Bible-inspired words of Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: BBB provides tips on buying Detroit Lions tickets, avoiding scams
item.description: The Better Business Bureau of West Michigan is providing some tips if you’re looking to buy tickets to the Detroit Lions game on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bunchman, NewsNation

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases heading to auction, including several Nevada locations
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chip Roy bid for Rules chair emerges as key issue ahead of Speaker vote
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy’s (R-Texas) bid to be chair of the House Rules Committee is emerging as a key issue for those withholding support for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) ahead of Friday afternoon’s Speaker election. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.), who has withheld support for Johnson, told former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans' trust in accuracy of US elections jumps after Trump's win, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Republicans say they are confident in the 2024 vote count after Donald Trump’s win, according to a new poll that finds a sharp turnaround from GOP voters’ skepticism about U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden says he will block US Steel sale to Nippon Steel
item.description: President Biden announced Friday he would block the sale of U.S. Steel to Japanese-owned Nippon Steel following a yearlong review of the potential acquisition. “We need major U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Cybertruck explosion marks second high-profile incident at Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: The Cybertruck fire and explosion was preceded by a 2021 bomb threat that was meant to serve as a message to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, WGN 9

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: FBI says driver in New Orleans rampage acted alone and was '100%' inspired by Islamic State group
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The Army veteran who drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers in New Orleans acted alone, the FBI said Thursday, reversing its position from a day earlier that he likely worked with others in the deadly attack that officials said was inspired by the Islamic State g
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A spectacular meteor shower is about to peak. How and when to watch
item.description: (KTLA) - Stargazers are in for a treat as the Quadrantids Meteor Shower, one of the year's best, reaches its peak over the Northern Hemisphere, according to NASA. This annual meteor shower is unique because it originates from an asteroid, 2003 EH1, rather than a comet.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 killed, 18 injured after plane crashes into warehouse in Southern California
item.description: Two people died and at least 18 people were injured Thursday afternoon when a plane crashed into the roof of a Southern California warehouse.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: National theater chain running $5 'Bring Your Own' popcorn bucket deal
item.description: A national theater chain is offering a one-day deal that will almost certainly lead to hijinks from moviegoers later this month. Cinemark has announced its first-ever “Bring Your Own Bucket” promotion as part of National Popcorn Day on Jan. 19.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the New Year's Day attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4
